title: Ways to Split Array Into Good Subarrays
content:
You are given a binary array nums.

A subarray of an array is good if it contains exactly one element with the value 1.

Return an integer denoting the number of ways to split the array nums into good subarrays. As the number may be too large, return it modulo 109 + 7.

A subarray is a contiguous non-empty sequence of elements within an array.

Example 1:

Input: nums = [0,1,0,0,1]
Output: 3
Explanation: There are 3 ways to split nums into good subarrays:
    - [0,1] [0,0,1]
    - [0,1,0] [0,1]
    - [0,1,0,0] [1]

Example 2:

Input: nums = [0,1,0]
Output: 1
Explanation: There is 1 way to split nums into good subarrays:
    - [0,1,0]

Constraints:

• 1 <= nums.length <= 105

• 0 <= nums[i] <= 1
hints: If the array consists of only 0s answer is 0.
In the final split, exactly one separation point exists between two consecutive 1s.
In how many ways can separation points be put?

solutions.explanation:
Each good subarray must contain exactly one 1. The zeros before the first 1 and after the last 1 are fixed (they must belong to the first and last subarray respectively).

Between two consecutive 1s at positions p and q, we must place exactly one split point somewhere in the gap (p, q) — that is, at any of the (q - p) positions between them. So there are (q - p) choices for each gap.

The total number of ways is the product of (ones[i] - ones[i-1]) for all consecutive 1-pairs, taken modulo 10^9+7.

If there are no 1s, no valid split exists and we return 0.

Time complexity: O(n). Space complexity: O(k) where k is the number of 1s.
